question: How do I configure Predictive Lead Scoring in Odoo CRM?
expected_answer: Go to CRM > Configuration > Settings. In the 'Update Probabilities' section, select the fields to use for scoring (country, state, email quality, phone quality, source, language, tags). Click Save. The system will start tracking conversion frequencies on won/lost transitions.

question: What is the difference between a Lead and an Opportunity in Odoo CRM?
expected_answer: A Lead is an unqualified potential customer with minimal information. An Opportunity is a qualified lead that has been converted with a defined expected revenue, probability, and pipeline stage. Leads can be automatically converted to opportunities or through the conversion wizard.

question: How do I set up sales teams in Odoo CRM?
expected_answer: Go to CRM > Configuration > Sales Teams. Create a new team with a name, members, and email alias. Configure lead assignment rules, team-specific stages, and revenue targets. Assign salespeople as team members with individual quotas.

question: How does IAP Lead Mining work in Odoo?
expected_answer: Lead Mining (crm_iap_mine) queries an external B2B database (DnB) to generate leads matching your criteria. You define filters: countries, industries, company size, roles. Each search costs IAP credits (1 credit/company + 1/contact). Results are imported as leads/opportunities.

question: How do I configure automatic lead enrichment?
expected_answer: Go to CRM > Settings. Under Lead Enrichment, select 'Enrich all leads automatically' or 'Enrich on demand only'. Automatic mode runs every 60 minutes via cron. Each enrichment costs 1 IAP credit and fetches company data from Clearbit based on email domain.

question: How do I set up UTM tracking for marketing campaigns?
expected_answer: UTM tracking uses the utm.mixin which provides campaign_id, medium_id, and source_id fields. Create UTM campaigns in Configuration > UTM Tags. When creating leads via website forms or email, URL parameters (utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium) are automatically captured and stored via cookies with 31-day expiry.

question: What factors influence the predictive lead scoring model and how can I customize them?
expected_answer: Default factors: phone_state, email_state, state_id, country_id, source_id, lang_id, tag_ids. Always included: stage_id, team_id. To customize: add records to crm.lead.scoring.frequency.field linked to ir.model.fields on crm.lead, then update crm.pls_fields config parameter. Run _cron_update_automated_probabilities() to rebuild the frequency table.

question: How do I handle duplicate leads in Odoo?
expected_answer: Odoo CRM automatically detects potential duplicates when creating leads with the same email or partner. The merge wizard (crm.merge.opportunity) allows combining duplicate leads into a single record, merging fields, activities, and communication history.

question: How do I configure rule-based lead assignment?
expected_answer: Enable Lead Auto-Assignment in CRM > Settings. Configure for each sales team: assignment domain (which leads they handle), assignment max (monthly capacity per member), and for each member: preference domain, opt-out flag, and individual capacity. The cron ir_cron_crm_lead_assign runs assignment logic.

question: What reports are available for CRM performance analysis?
expected_answer: Key reports: Pipeline Analysis (win/loss by stage), Expected Revenue Report, Forecast Report, Quality Leads Report, Lead Distribution Report, Marketing Attribution Reports, Opportunity Analysis (pivot/graph), Activity Report, and CRM Dashboard with KPIs.
